headline: By writing to Putin, Zelensky addresses the Russian elites who are weary of the war in Ukraine
description: Behind the offer of an outstretched hand, the Ukrainian president's letter to the Russian leader seeks to exploit Moscow's vulnerabilities and the Russian people's exhaustion after four years of high-intensity war. Zelensky's initiative comes as his European partners are attempting to regain control of peace talks that the Trump administration abandoned.

headline: US strikes radar installations in Iran
DNA test confirms body found in France is that of missing 11 year-old girl
Ever-extreme Eric Zemmour prepares second bid for French president
description: For a time, Zemmour's partner, Sarah Knafo, had positioned herself as a potential candidate for the couple's party, Reconquête. But the far-right polemicist is claiming the limelight again.

headline: Mali's ruling junta sentences a French agent to 20 years in prison
description: Yann V., who was officially registered as an agent of France's foreign intelligence service (DGSE) with Malian authorities, was arrested in August 2025 in Bamako, accused of having attempted to orchestrate a 'destabilization attempt' against the junta led by General Assimi Goïta.

headline: Did Rafael Jodar really push that ball girl? That's beside the point
description: Whether the Spanish tennis player did or did not shove a young volunteer ball girl during the Roland-Garros tournament hardly matters; for many social media users, what counts most is chiming in.

headline: Over three decades, China's promise became a trap for European industry
description: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (1/4). In just a few decades, China has gone from being the world's factory to a major technological rival for Europeans, who had hoped to profit on China's vast domestic market but underestimated its capacity for innovation.
byline: Bastien Bonnefous,  Isabelle Chaperon and  Sophie Fay

headline: US judge blocks Trump restrictions on legal immigration
US envoy confirms Iranian footballers granted visas for World Cup
As Israeli bombs target Lebanon, Tyre residents call for the ancient city to be declared an 'open city' and demilitarized
Israel's government steps up attacks on judiciary: 'A race to eliminate democratic institutions has begun'
French prosecutors open 'war crime' and 'torture' probe over Israel's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists
Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution' rises up against the Trump family and Prime Minister Edi Rama
Bold new proposal calls for merging Paris with suburbs
Seoul's Centre Pompidou Hanwha aims to build a cultural bridge between France and South Korea
War in Ukraine: Russia fired Oreshnik missile on city  occupied by its own forces
The health of French presidents, a state secret: 'Illness is not honorable; it's better not to speak of it'
These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
Michelin guide transforms itself to become global benchmark for the art of living
Alone in Beijing: How Chinese dissidents challenge the regime
description: 'Resisting a repressive regime' (2/3). Starting in 2020, China's zero-Covid policy and strict lockdowns triggered widespread protests, fueling a political awakening among young people. As the authorities tightened control, some have responded with passive resistance.

headline: Missing girl of 11: Six questions on possible failings of the French justice system
description: Jérôme B. is suspected of having abducted Lyhanna, who has been missing for a week. Did the justice system overlook past accusations against him? Did the courts' backlog lead to potential delays in the investigations?

headline: Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
description: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.
byline: Jacques Mandelbaum,  Frédéric Potet and  Roxana Azimi

headline: Europe's painful awakening on digital sovereignty
Pascal Ory, historian: Edgar Morin's 'acute sensitivity to the spirit of the times made him a trailblazer'
'The current war between Iran and the US stems from decades of mutual humiliation'
